breakClass: .ToList()/.ToArray() (or a nested correlated-collection projection) embedded inside a server-side EF Core 10 query â€” either in a `let` clause whose value is consumed by a server `select new {...}`/`where ... .Contains(...)`, or as an inline `Field = (from ... let X=db....ToList() ... select ...).ToList()` sub-collection â€” on an outer IQueryable that has NOT yet crossed an .AsEnumerable()/.ToList() materialization boundary. EF6 client-evaluated these; EF Core 10 throws "could not be translated" (500). Outer queries that are materialized first (var data2 = v.ToList(); from o in data2 ...) or sourced from db.X.AsEnumerable() are SAFE.
why: Exact mirror of ProTaskController lines 370/392: un-materialized db.Amcs / db.ProTasks sources, let assign = db....ToList() consumed by where assign.Contains(EmpId) -> embedded .ToList() that EF Core 10 cannot translate. Port regression. Two breaks (227 and 249).
finalFix:
In QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs, method `mapviewshow`:

FIX 1 â€” `amc` query (assign used only in the where, so drop the let):
- Delete line 227: `let assign = db.PeriodicMaintAssignedToes.Where(x => x.PeriodicMaintDtlId == d.PeriodicMaintDetailsId && x.Status == "Assigned" && x.ChkStatus == Status.active).Select(x => x.EmployeeId).ToList()`
- Change line 231 from:
  `(editperiodic==true||assign.Contains(EmpId))`
  to:
  `(editperiodic==true || db.PeriodicMaintAssignedToes.Any(x => x.PeriodicMaintDtlId == d.PeriodicMaintDetailsId && x.Status == "Assigned" && x.ChkStatus == Status.active && x.EmployeeId == EmpId))`

FIX 2 â€” `task` query (assign is ALSO projected at line 264 and used client-side at line 276, so KEEP the let; only change the where):
- Keep line 249 `let assign = ...` unchanged.
- Change line 253 from:
  `(editprotask == true || assign.Contains(EmpId))`
  to:
  `(editprotask == true || db.TaskAssigneds.Any(x => x.ProTaskId == a.ProTaskId && x.Status == "Assigned" && x.chkStatus == Status.active && x.EmployeeId == EmpId))`

This replaces the un-translatable per-row `.ToList()` + `.Contains` predicate with a SQL EXISTS (`.Any()`), which EF Core 10 translates and which preserves the exact legacy boolean (show row if user can edit-all OR is an active "Assigned" assignee). Output is byte-identical.

NOTE 1: For the `task` query, after removing the predicate dependency, the surviving `let assign` is now used only in the final `select new { ... assign ... }` (line 264) which is materialized at `.ToList()` (line 267) and consumed client-side at line 276 â€” EF Core 10 supports correlated collection projections in a terminal Select, so leaving it there is safe. If that terminal collection projection also fails at runtime, replace the `let`/`assign` projection with a post-materialization lookup, but the predicate fix above is what resolves the flagged 500.

NOTE 2: Apply the identical fix to the Branch==1 twin in QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs (drop let at line 370 + rewrite line 374's `assign.Contains(EmpId)` to the PeriodicMaintAssignedToes `.Any()` EXISTS; keep let at line 392 + rewrite line 396's `assign.Contains(EmpId)` to the TaskAssigneds `.Any()` EXISTS). This twin has the same unfixed break and will 500 on the same endpoint for Branch 1.
reasoning:
I independently confirmed the flag at QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs, method `mapviewshow` (the geomap/map-points endpoint, starts line 161). Two breaks:

(1) Line 227/231 â€” `var amc = (from a in db.Amcs join b ... join d ... join c ... let assign = db.PeriodicMaintAssignedToes.Where(x => x.PeriodicMaintDtlId == d.PeriodicMaintDetailsId && x.Status=="Assigned" && x.ChkStatus==Status.active).Select(x=>x.EmployeeId).ToList() where ... && (editperiodic==true||assign.Contains(EmpId)) ... select new points {...}).ToList();`

(2) Line 249/253 â€” `var task = (from a in db.ProTasks.Where(o=>o.Branch==2) join c ... let assign = db.TaskAssigneds.Where(x => x.ProTaskId==a.ProTaskId && x.Status=="Assigned" && x.chkStatus==Status.active).Select(x=>x.EmployeeId).ToList() where ... && (editprotask==true||assign.Contains(EmpId)) ... select new {... assign ...}).ToList()...`

SERVER-SIDE confirmed: the outer sources are raw DbSets (`db.Amcs`, `db.ProTasks.Where(...)`). I grepped the whole method â€” the only `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()` boundaries are the TRAILING ones at line 244 (amc) and 267 (task). No materialization precedes them, so the `let assign = subquery.ToList()` and its `assign.Contains(EmpId)` consumption in the `where` are composed into the un-materialized IQueryable. EmployeeId is `long` (non-nullable) on both PeriodicMaintAssignedTo (Models/AMC.cs:216) and TaskAssigned (Models/ProTask.cs:325); EmpId is `long` (db.Employees...Select(EmployeeId).FirstOrDefault()), so `assign` is `List<long>` and the predicate is `List<long>.Contains(long)`.

WHY it 500s: A `let assign = db.X.Where(...).Select(...).ToList()` builds a per-outer-row correlated collection via an embedded `.ToList()`, and `assign.Contains(EmpId)` probes that materialized list inside a server-side WHERE. EF Core 10 cannot translate an in-query `.ToList()` correlated subquery consumed by `.Contains` in a predicate â€” it throws InvalidOperationException "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated" at query-compile time, BEFORE any rows are read. So it 500s unconditionally (independent of data), not just on edge data. EF6 client-evaluated this, so it worked in the legacy app â€” a genuine PORT REGRESSION.

PORT REGRESSION confirmed: the ported code is byte-for-byte identical to legacy _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs (let-assign at legacy line 222 / 244). This is the batch-13b "collection-projection / embedded-ToList" class documented in MEMORY (reports-audit-batch24-26). The parallel ProTaskController.mapviewshow (lines 370/392, Branch==1) has the IDENTICAL break and is ALSO unfixed (git shows batch-26 ea4f19c only touched ProTask/GetAllTaskdashexp, not mapviewshow; both controllers still contain 4 `let assign = db` occurrences). No try/catch wraps the query; it flows into `var customers`/`return View`, so the exception surfaces as HTTP 500. Severity HIGH (translation failure fires on any request, not just edge data).

One refinement vs the hunter's proposed fix: in the `task` query, `assign` is reused AFTER the where â€” projected at line 264 and consumed client-side at line 276 (after `.ToList()` line 267). So the `let` cannot be blindly dropped there; only the WHERE consumption (line 253) must change to `.Any()` EXISTS, leaving the `let` for the terminal projection. In the `amc` query, `assign` is used only in the where (line 231), so its `let` can be dropped entirely.

The same two-line fix must also be applied to ProTaskController.cs lines 370/374 and 392/396 (Branch==1 twin).

breakClass: DateTime arithmetic helper inside an unmaterialized query: DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays/AddMinutes (plain CLR helpers in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs, NOT registered EF functions) applied to a COLUMN expression inside a server-side LINQ-to-Entities projection or Where. EF6 used DbFunctions.AddDays (canonical -> SQL DATEADD); the port swapped in DbFunctionsCompat.* which EF Core 10 cannot translate, so the query throws 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Fix = EF-native (col).Value.AddDays(n ?? 0) which DOES translate to DATEADD.
why: Identical to ProTaskController.Getmanhour: select new of unmaterialized db.servicereports query, .ToList() boundary at L6590 after projection -> server-side. CLR helper untranslatable -> 500. Legacy DbFunctions.AddDays. Port regression.
finalFix:
In Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs line 6588, replace the untranslatable CLR helper with the EF-native nullable-DateTime AddDays, which EF Core 10 translates to SQL DATEADD and preserves the legacy DateTime? result/null-handling:

    endtime = (a.endtime < a.starttime) ? a.endtime.Value.AddDays(1) : a.endtime

This mirrors EF6's DbFunctions.AddDays(a.endtime, 1). `.Value` is safe here because the ternary branch is only taken when `a.endtime < a.starttime`, which already requires endtime to be non-null. The result type stays DateTime? (the false branch returns the nullable a.endtime), so the downstream `.ToList().Select(...)` that does `(o.endtime - o.starttime).Value...` is unchanged. Apply the IDENTICAL fix to the byte-for-byte-duplicate ProTaskController.Getmanhour at Controllers/ProTaskController.cs line 7039.
reasoning:
I tried to refute but the claim holds on every axis. LOCATION: Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs line 6588, method Getmanhour (signature L6547). The flagged expression: `endtime = (a.endtime < a.starttime) ? DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(a.endtime, 1) : a.endtime`.

SERVER-SIDE â€” CONFIRMED. The query (L6577-6600) is `var data = (from a in db.servicereports join b in db.servicereportmembers ... join c in db.Employees ... where a.protaskid==protaskid select new { ... endtime = (...)? DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(a.endtime,1) : a.endtime }).ToList().Select(o => new {...})`. The opening `(` is on L6577 and the matching `)` is immediately before `.ToList()` on L6590. The `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays` call lives INSIDE the `select new` projection (L6581-6589) which is part of the IQueryable being enumerated by that `.ToList()`. So the fragment is UPSTREAM of the first materialization boundary â€” genuinely server-side. I verified there is no earlier `.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToList()`: `db.servicereports` (DbSet, ApplicationDbContext.cs L67) and `db.servicereportmembers` (L68) and `db.Employees` are real DbSets, so this is a true LINQ-to-Entities IQueryable, not an in-memory list. It is NOT a group-aggregate over an IGrouping and NOT a standalone top-level FirstOrDefault.

WOULD 500 â€” CONFIRMED. DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays is a plain unregistered CLR static helper: `public static DateTime? AddDays(DateTime? date, int? days) => date?.AddDays(days ?? 0);` (Helpers/SendMailShim.cs L70). EF Core 10 cannot translate an arbitrary CLR static method on a column expression inside a server-side projection, so translating this `select new` for `.ToList()` throws InvalidOperationException 'The LINQ expression ... could not be translated' â†’ unhandled â†’ HTTP 500. This fails at translation time, independent of data, so it 500s on the very first realistic call (HIGH severity), not just edge data.

PORT REGRESSION â€” CONFIRMED. Legacy EF6 at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskNonTechController.cs L7002 used `endtime = (a.endtime < a.starttime) ? DbFunctions.AddDays(a.endtime, 1) : a.endtime` â€” DbFunctions.AddDays is the canonical EF6 function that EF6 translated to SQL DATEADD and ran fine. The port swapped in the untranslatable DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays. Entity types: servicereport.endtime and .starttime are both DateTime? (Models/ProTask.cs L34-35). Note: this method is byte-for-byte identical to ProTaskController.Getmanhour (ProTaskController.cs L7028-7051, AddDays at L7039) â€” the same fix applies to both.
